subject: Geology
title: Fossils in Rock
description: A micro-lesson that makes Fossils in Rock usable.

hook.text: A fish died 380 million years ago, sank to the bottom, and turned to stone. Its skeleton is still readable today.

buildup.text: Fossils form when organisms are buried in sediment before they decay. Minerals slowly replace the original tissue.

discovery.text: Permineralization, molds, casts, and trace fossils each preserve different aspects of ancient life.

twist.text: Fossilization is extremely rare. Less than one percent of all species that ever lived left any fossil record.

climax.text: Fossils connect rocks to biology. They date layers, reveal past climates, and track the evolution of life.

quiz.question: Why is fossilization considered rare?
options: Less than one percent of species left fossil records
Fossils only form in volcanic rock
